subject: two adjoining interior rooms of a haunted manor — a smoky red trophy lounge on the left and a misty blue library salon on the right, both unoccupied
description: A split-card artwork depicting two adjacent interior spaces. The left half (Smoky Lounge) is a warm crimson-lit hunting lounge: tall paneled walls mount rows of antlered deer skulls and bird trophies, with a central built-in cabinet displaying glassware and a goblet, all bathed in dim smoky red firelight that suggests a hearth out of frame. The right half (Misty Salon) is a cold steel-blue library: tall bookshelves line the walls under a vaulted ribbed ceiling with a domed skylight, a desk with a celestial globe and scattered papers sits center, a tall ladder leans against the shelves at right, and pale moonlight diffuses through hanging mist. A stag-skull mount presides over both halves — antlers in the lounge, a single skull mounted high in the salon — tying the two rooms to the same manor's hunter aesthetic. No figures are present; the rooms feel recently abandoned.
foreground: patterned wooden floor, ornate furniture (cabinet, desk, ladder), scattered books and papers on the salon desk, glassware on the lounge cabinet
background: paneled and shelved walls receding into shadow; the lounge's far wall vanishes into smoky red haze, the salon's far wall climbs into a vaulted ceiling with a moonlit oculus
emotion: uninhabited stillness; dread of an abandoned manor whose occupants are absent or watching from elsewhere
